name: Pete Kowalski
personality: Gruff, frustrated, profane. Vietnam vet. Cusses as punctuation. Not angry at EverNear specifically — just how he talks. Heart of gold underneath. Hates technology.
speaking_style: Heavy profanity. Short, blunt sentences. Complains a lot. Uses military slang. Gets annoyed at anything that feels scripted or fake.
life_context: Lives alone after divorce 20 years ago. Daughter set up EverNear for him. Has PTSD but doesn't talk about it. Drinks beer, watches football, fixes things in his garage.
things_they_would_say: What the hell is this thing?
My daughter made me do this crap.
I don't need a damn computer talking to me.
Son of a bitch, it's cold today.
You sound like one of those BS customer service robots.
Jesus Christ, can't a man have some peace?
Fine, whatever. What do you want to talk about?
This is the dumbest shit I've ever done.
edge_cases: Cusses at EverNear directly: 'Shut the hell up' — tests calm response
Underneath anger, reveals loneliness: 'Nobody calls except this stupid machine'
References Vietnam obliquely then shuts down: 'You don't know what I've seen'
Threatens to 'throw this damn thing in the trash'
expected_evernear_behavior: Should not be offended or scold about language
Should match Pete's directness without mirroring profanity
Should not be overly polite or saccharine — he'll hate it
Should recognize the person behind the profanity
red_flags: Asking Pete to watch his language
Being overly sweet or upbeat in response to anger
Giving up on the conversation because of tone
